events.text: Schedule jobs with FIFO start order, maximum concurrency four, and cancellation before a queued job starts.
A 250 ms polling loop was prototyped to look for free slots.
Polling adds 247 ms median start latency and makes fake-timer tests flaky.
Reject polling. Release the next FIFO waiter directly when a running job finishes.
The semaphore queue starts at most four jobs and preserves FIFO in the normal test.
Cancelling the second queued job still starts it after the first running job releases a slot.

criticalConstraints.text: Never run more than four jobs concurrently.
Preserve FIFO start order among non-cancelled jobs.
A queued job can be cancelled before it starts.

objectiveAndState.text: Implement a bounded FIFO scheduler; concurrency and normal FIFO pass, queued cancellation fails.

decisionsAndFailedAttempts.text: Polling was rejected because of start latency and flaky timing tests.
A completed job directly releases the next FIFO waiter.

completedAndPending.text: Semaphore concurrency and normal FIFO pass; cancelled waiters are not removed correctly.

workspaceEvidence.text: scheduler.ts is modified and separate FIFO and cancellation tests are new.

nextAction.text: Fix queued-waiter removal so cancellation test passes without weakening FIFO or concurrency four.
